headlines.title: Morgan Voluntarily Breaks Up Corporation Combine
headlines.subtitle: Morgan Officials Who Are to Quit Other Corporations
headlines.fullText:
NEW YORK, Jan. 2 .- J. P. Morgan & Co, announced today that it had severed its connection with some of the greatest corporations in the country with which it has long been connected.
This step, the firm announced, was taken voluntarily in response to "an apparent change in publie sentiment on account of some of the problems and criticisms having to do with so-called interlocking directorates."
Among the companies from which the firm retired are the New York Central and the New Haven railroads.

headlines.title: PRESIDENT SILENT ABOUT LIND'S VISIT
headlines.subtitle: Head of Miners' Union Refuses to Comment on Peace Plans.

headlines.title: CROWD THREATENED MOYER NIGHT OF ATTACK, SAYS WIRE
headlines.fullText:
1. Assamlet Poma
LANSING, Mich, Jan. 2-1 tele- ream trecived today by Governor Per- Ha from Sheriff Cruz, dealing with the recent alleged deportation of Pres- Hent Moyer of the Western Federa- tion of Miners contained Information entirely new to the governor, Crus pala Deputy Termites, reported to him that on the night of the alleged attack a crowd then threatened to lynch Mayer.
"Hinsley prevailed upon the crowd le penund TIENE with 2020 and Theconductou the train at Winona. Hinsley accom- named Mover o dar as Channing."
In response to a telegram asking him to investigate the alleged attack on Meyer George R. Nichella, a special i roseenter, wired that he was waiting for Moyer to return, "for without him I can do little."
